instruction: What removal spell should I play in a white Legacy deck?
output: Swords to Plowshares is the gold standard — W for instant-speed exile of any creature. No other white removal comes close in efficiency. After that: Solitude provides free exile by pitching a white card. Prismatic Ending hits any nonland permanent with MV equal to colors of mana spent. Council's Judgment exiles any nonland permanent and ignores hexproof and protection. March of Otherworldly Light exiles artifacts, creatures, or enchantments. All white. Do not look to other colors — white has the best creature removal in Legacy.

instruction: What are the best blue card selection spells in Legacy?
output: Brainstorm (U, instant, draw 3 put 2 back) is the best — combined with fetchlands it's effectively draw 3. Ponder (U, sorcery, look at top 3, may shuffle) is second — always paired with Brainstorm. Preordain (U, sorcery, scry 2 then draw 1) is a distant third but still playable. Consider Bolas's Citadel in some lists. For countermagic: Force of Will (free), Daze (free), Force of Negation (free on opponent's turn), Spell Pierce (U), Flusterstorm (U, storm copies). All blue. Blue's card selection is why it is the dominant color in Legacy.

instruction: What are the best black disruption spells for Legacy?
output: Thoughtseize (B, take any nonland card, lose 2 life) is the best — surgical hand disruption for 1 mana. Orcish Bowmasters (1B, flash creature, punishes cantrips) doubles as disruption and a threat. Fatal Push (B, destroys creature with MV 2 or less, or 4 with revolt) is the best cheap black removal. Snuff Out (free with a Swamp, destroys nonblack creature for 4 life) gives free removal. Hymn to Tourach (BB, opponent discards 2 random) is devastating but random. Toxic Deluge (2B, all creatures get -X/-X) is the best black board wipe.

instruction: What are the best red cards for an aggressive Legacy deck?
output: Lightning Bolt (R, 3 damage to any target) is the benchmark. Rite of Flame (R, add RR) for Storm. Pyroblast (R, counter blue spell or destroy blue permanent) is essential sideboard. Blood Moon (2R, nonbasic lands become Mountains) punishes greedy mana bases. Broadside Bombardiers sees play in Mardu Energy. For creatures: Dragon's Rage Channeler (R, 1/1 that grows with delirium), Goblin Welder (R, swap artifacts) in Painter. Red excels at direct damage, mana denial, and artifact synergies.

Green is less dominant than blue or black but has strong niche cards. Endurance (2G, flash, shuffles graveyard — free via evoke) is premium graveyard hate. Collector Ouphe (1G, shuts off artifacts) is a powerful sideboard card. Life from the Loam (1G, dredge back lands) is the engine of the Lands deck. Exploration (G, play extra lands) enables Lands' fast starts. Green Sun's Zenith (XG, tutor a green creature) provides a creature toolbox. Tarmogoyf/Barrowgoyf in BG shells. Green's strength is in creature-based strategies and land-focused decks.

instruction: What are the best sideboard cards against graveyard decks in Legacy?
output: Leyline of the Void (free if in opening hand, exiles all opponent's graveyard cards) is the strongest — 36% of decks play it. Surgical Extraction (free, exile all copies of a specific card from everywhere) is precision removal. Endurance (free via evoke, shuffles opponent's graveyard into library) is the best green option. Tormod's Crypt (0 mana artifact, sacrifice to exile a graveyard) is the cheapest option. Grafdigger's Cage (1 mana artifact, prevents cards in graveyards from entering play) stops Reanimator and Dread Return. Rest in Peace (1W, exiles all graveyards permanently) if you're in white.

instruction: What cards should I put in my sideboard to beat combo?
output: Depends on your colors. Blue: Force of Will (main), Force of Negation, Flusterstorm (stops Storm cold), Mindbreak Trap (free vs Storm). Black: Thoughtseize (main), Surgical Extraction. White: Thalia Guardian of Thraben (taxes all spells), Deafening Silence (1 noncreature per turn), Containment Priest (exiles cheated creatures), Ethersworn Canonist (1 nonartifact per turn). Red: Pyroblast (counters blue combo enablers), Red Elemental Blast. Colorless: Chalice of the Void (counters specific MV), Trinisphere (everything costs 3+). Pick based on your colors and which combo decks you expect.

instruction: What are the core cards for a UW control deck in Legacy?
output: The UW control shell in Legacy: Card selection: 4 Brainstorm, 4 Ponder. Countermagic: 4 Force of Will, 2-3 Counterspell, 1-2 Force of Negation. Removal: 4 Swords to Plowshares, 1-2 Prismatic Ending, 1-2 Council's Judgment. Threats: 2-3 Monastery Mentor or Shark Typhoon. Card advantage: 2-3 Up the Beanstalk (if running pitch spells). Lands: Tundra (or Hallowed Fountain on budget), fetchlands, basics. The key to UW is that every card is white or blue — no off-color splashes diluting the mana base.

instruction: What cards should be in a mono-red Legacy deck?
output: Mono-red in Legacy means either Burn or Red Stompy. Burn: 4 Lightning Bolt, 4 Chain Lightning, 4 Rift Bolt, 4 Lava Spike, 4 Price of Progress (devastating vs nonbasics), 4 Eidolon of the Great Revel, 4 Goblin Guide. Red Stompy/Prison: 4 Chalice of the Void, 4 Blood Moon, 2-3 Trinisphere, 4 Ancient Tomb, 4 City of Traitors, 3-4 Chrome Mox, threats like Fury, Caves of Chaos Adventurer, or Broadside Bombardiers. All cards are red or colorless — no other colors needed.
